name: Pastry for 9-inch One Crust Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the shortening into the flour and salt until the mixture consists of small, pea-sized particles.
2. Sprinkle in cold water, about 1 tablespoon at a time, tossing with a fork until all the flour is moistened and the pastry is almost smooth on the side of the bowl (up to 2 teaspoons of water may be added if necessary).
3. Gather the pastry into a ball and shape it into a flattened round on a lightly floured cloth-covered board.
4. Roll the pastry 9 inches in diameter and approximately 1 1/4 inches tall, larger than the inverted pie plate.
5. Fold the pastry into quarters and place it in the plate.
6. Unfold the pastry and press firmly against the bottom and sides of the plate.
7. Trim the edge of the pastry about 1 inch from the rim of the plate.
8. Fold and roll the pastry under, even with the plate, and flute.
